title: Sepsis Bundle Trigger And Tracking
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: sepsis bundle trigger and tracking.
assumptions: Single primary physician is assigned to a case at a time in this model; multi-provider splits are out of scope.
Resource availability predicates (e.g., bed_available, antibiotic_pack_available) are authoritative and updated atomically by reservation/release actions.
Timing and quantitative dosing details (e.g., ml of fluid, minutes elapsed) are abstracted; the model represents only discrete state transitions and resource allocations.
Subtypes of encounters (adult/pediatric) capture routing constraints but do not model clinical detail differences beyond compatibility checks.
risks: Simplified resource model may underrepresent concurrent demand and queuing effects; in reality contention may lead to delays not captured here.
Safety-critical timing constraints (e.g., antibiotics within 1 hour) are not enforced by temporal predicates in this template and must be handled by an external scheduler or policy layer.
Mapping ambiguity between similar clinical roles (e.g., 'nursing_staff' vs 'bedside_sepsis_nurse') can lead to misassignment if real deployments conflate roles.
Clinical complexity (contraindications, allergies, weight-based dosing) is not encoded; applying this blueprint without those checks could risk unsafe recommendations.
